stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 4 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability between 72% and 88% (uncertain due to limited operational data). test 2 has a reliability of 81%. test 3 has a reliability of 93%. test 4 has a reliability of 81%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all tests simultaneously, estimated between 3.3% and 7.0%. What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9293 to 0.9667. Point estimate: 0.9475.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 tests fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (3.3%-7.0%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9293, 0.9667].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
